title: West Nile Virus found in mosquito test sample in Jamaica Plain
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: West Nile Virus found in a mosquito sample in Jamaica Plain, marking the first positive test in Boston this summer.
Boston Public Health Commission advises precautions despite low transmission risk, such as eliminating standing water and using insect repellent.
No human cases of West Nile Virus confirmed; symptoms can include fever, headache, and nausea, with severe cases rare.

title: Boston man accused of making homemade explosives, threats on MBTA Commuter Rail
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Shane Cautillo, 29, charged with hoax incendiary device possession and disorderly conduct on MBTA Commuter Rail.
Police responded to Cautillo's threats involving bombs and explosives expressed aboard a Commuter Rail train at North Station.
Cautillo prohibited from using MBTA and held on $10,000 bail; scheduled for July 28 pretrial hearing.

title: Lawsuit: ICE detained East Boston father despite legal status
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jose Pineda, a legally resident Salvadoran immigrant in the U.S., is suing ICE for unlawful arrest and detention.
Pineda's lawsuit claims racial discrimination and inhumane detention conditions during a two-day ICE custody in Burlington.
ICE initiated removal proceedings against Pineda despite his humanitarian protections, which were ultimately dismissed.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Running backs edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson lead a Patriots' running back group ranked sixth in NFL rushing yards per game.
Henderson set a franchise rookie record with 5.1 yards per carry and was a finalist for offensive rookie of the year.
The Patriots face depth concerns at running back, adding Jam Miller in the seventh round and Reggie Gilliam as fullback.

title: Boston cyclist, a city employee, hit and killed by vehicle on Tremont Street
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A bicyclist was fatally struck by a vehicle on Tremont Street in Mission Hill, Boston, Thursday morning.
The victim, Louisa Gag, a Boston city employee, was pronounced dead at the scene near Roxbury Crossing.
Boston Police closed Tremont Street from Brigham Circle to Roxbury Crossing, reopening it around noon after investigation.

title: Massachusetts Senate passes bill targeting addictive social media features for kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Senate passed bill S.3164 targeting social media features deemed addictive for minors, with 38 votes in favor.
The legislation disables autoplay, infinite scroll, and algorithmic feeds for minors, with additional privacy and time-use measures.
Bill maintains minors' access to social platforms while ensuring companies report targeting strategies to the Attorney General annually.

title: Police seize dog who severely injured smaller pup in Chelsea attack caught on video
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Chelsea police have seized and quarantined an unlicensed dog after it seriously injured a smaller pup in an attack.
The injured dog, a service animal named Snoopy Snoop Dog, is set to have its leg amputated following the incident.
Police are investigating potential violations of local ordinances and state laws, including Chelsea’s leash law, from the attack.

title: Mac Jones opens up about his tumultuous time with the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Mac Jones was traded from the Patriots to the Jaguars for a sixth-round pick after a rocky tenure.
Jones cited the departure of offensive coordinator Josh McDaniels as the start of the Patriots' offensive struggles.
Jones described challenges in play-calling dynamics with Bill Belichick, Matt Patricia, and Joe Judge during his time.

title: 5 takeaways as France defeat Morocco in the last ‘Boston Stadium’ World Cup game, advance to semifinals
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in the World Cup quarterfinal at Gillette Stadium, advancing to the semifinals.
Goals were scored by Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembélé, overcoming Moroccan defensive efforts.
Boston bids farewell to World Cup hosting duties as Gillette Stadium transitions back to hosting local events.

title: ‘He hit me’: Pregame France vs. Morocco press conference briefly halted after journalist altercation
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: A pregame press conference in Boston for France vs. Morocco was briefly halted due to a journalist altercation.
The altercation involved accusations of physical contact; FIFA officials intervened to calm the situation.
Moroccan player Brahim Díaz resumed the conference with humor, saying, 'I forgot the question.'
